title: pedicularis verticillata var. caespitosa
intro: Pedicularis verticillata var. caespitosa, also known as Whorled Lousewort, is a plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits. Whorled Lousewort can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
